input.background: Exercise 11.2 included survival times of 29 patients with lactic acidosis, together with some possibly prognostic variables.
input.question: What problem is there with these data regarding a Kaplan-Meier plot of survival?

output.answer: Because the longest survival time is so much greater than the next longest, the meaningful early part of the curve will be severely compressed at the left hand side. The graph is much more useful if this patient is excluded. As noted in section 13.8.2, stopping the curve when there are only five patients still alive will usually give a more reliable visual impression.
